name: Cefotaxime
description: Cefotaxime is a third-generation cephalosporin antibiotic. Like other third-generation cephalosporins, it has broad spectrum activity against Gram positive and Gram negative bacteria. In most cases, it is considered to be equivalent to ceftriaxone in terms of safety and efficacy. Cefotaxime sodium is marketed under various trade names including Claforan (Sanofi-Aventis).
indication: Used to treat gonorrhoea, meningitis, and severe infections including infections of the kidney (pyelonephritis) and urinary system. Also used before an operation to prevent infection after surgery.
toxicity: Adverse effects following overdosage include nausea, vomiting, epigastric distress, diarrhea, and convulsions. Oral rat LD<sub>50</sub> is over 20,000 mg/kg while intravenous rat LD<sub>50</sub> is over 7,000 mg/kg.
mechanismOfAction: The bactericidal activity of cefotaxime results from the inhibition of cell wall synthesis via affinity for penicillin-binding proteins (PBPs). Cefotaxime shows high affinity for penicillin-binding proteins in the cell wall including PBP Ib and PBP III.
pharmacodynamics: Cefotaxime is a third generation intravenous cephalosporin antibiotic. It has broad spectrum activity against Gram positive and Gram negative bacteria. It does not have activity against Pseudomonas aeruginosa. Cefotaxime works by inhibiting bacterial cell wall biosynthesis. A positive feature of cefotaxime is that it display a resistance to penicillinases and is useful to treat infections that are resistant to penicillin derivatives.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefotaxime.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefotaxime.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefotaxime can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefotaxime may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.
